materialChanges: Corrected the scalar/vector boundary, generalized component arithmetic, norm, normalization, and dot product to three dimensions, defined atan2 argument order and quadrant correction, and added worked dot-product and scalar-projection reasoning.
Rebuilt the add-magnitudes misconception as a prediction-to-recheck repair sequence and stated the triangle-inequality equality condition including zero-vector cases.

Made the magnitude assessment require and score a sum-of-squares norm method, and replaced the routine transfer scene and item with a changed-angle-convention audit whose arithmetic and feedback were recomputed.

Added scalar/vector and triangle-inequality claims, narrowed overmapped claim scenes, removed the unsupported distance/displacement wording, and broadened the existing NIST record to exact §§1.2(v) and 1.6(i) locators without adding a source.

Added static, print, and screen-reader retrieval instructions, reduced the declared scene-time total from 240 to 90 minutes, and corrected attribution so author, reviewer, and finalizer provenance point to their respective artifacts.

reviewDispositions.rationale: The prompt now explicitly requires a sum-of-squares norm calculation, the two-point magnitude rubric requires that valid method as well as the value 7, and the common-error text identifies the coincidental signed-sum match. The false shortcut can no longer earn full credit.

Concept prose and glossary now define a scalar as one possibly signed real value and a vector through vector addition, scalar multiplication, and component representation; they state that every Euclidean vector has a magnitude but only a nonzero vector has a unique direction.

The unsupported distance/displacement wording was removed. New scalar/vector and triangle-inequality claims now map every scene where those ideas are taught or assessed, same-scene source notes expose them, and the existing NIST record was broadened to exact §§1.2(v) and 1.6(i) locators supporting vectors, norms, normalization, Cauchy–Schwarz equality, and the triangle inequality.

The misconception repair now limits the same-direction equality condition to two nonzero vectors, states the positive-scalar-multiple criterion, and separately records equality when either vector is zero.

claim-dot-product no longer maps the transfer scene; its worked-example mapping is now supported by a substantive dot-product and projection example. claim-direction-components and the matching source-note IDs were removed from misconception and practice, while retained mappings correspond to actual learner-visible use.

The review independently confirmed the original numerical key and other arithmetic. Those verified values needed no correction; the transfer item was later replaced for learning-design reasons and its new values were independently recomputed in this run.

The misconception block now elicits and commits a prediction, neutrally names the shortcut, tests it with two counterexamples, explains why magnitudes lose direction, repairs the model with component addition and the triangle inequality, and ends with a new recheck plus feedback.

The concept scene now gives three-dimensional addition, subtraction, scalar multiplication, Euclidean magnitude, normalization, and dot product, and explicitly explains that the two-dimensional forms omit the z component.

The worked-example scene now calculates a dot product, interprets its positive sign and included angle, verifies a unit direction, calculates a signed scalar projection, and interprets its sign before independent practice and assessment.

The transfer scene and corresponding assessment now require detecting and adapting a clockwise-from-positive-vertical convention instead of repeating the standard horizontal-axis routine; the learner must diagnose a plausible swap error, translate the representation, and audit the result.

The concept scene now states that atan2 takes the vertical component first and horizontal component second, explains the 180-degree and 360-degree corrections for a 0-to-360-degree convention, handles a zero horizontal component, and preserves the zero-vector boundary. The worked check applies the 180-degree correction explicitly.
Practice now begins with instructions to pause and commit an answer before reading feedback, with explicit routes for print and screen-reader use.
Scene estimates were recalibrated to 10, 20, 15, 10, 15, 10, and 10 minutes, preserving their exact sum while reducing the lesson total from 240 to 90 minutes.

evidenceChecked: Confirmed authoritative scene order, exactly one level-one heading per scene, semantic headings and lists, and a continuous source reading order that does not depend on viewport position.

Confirmed every essential vector relationship is available in words, signed components, and equations; the pack has no images, diagrams, audio, video, color-only encoding, hover, drag, motion, or timed interaction requiring an alternative.
Confirmed retrieval and practice can be completed by writing or speaking, with explicit print and screen-reader pause instructions, repeatable feedback, retakes, and recovery guidance.

Confirmed no raw HTML, HTML entities, JavaScript, remote embeds, executable content, unsafe local paths, secrets, personal data, patient material, clinical advice, or credential claim appears in the final pack.

Inspected the single NIST source record, exact claim locators, factual-reference use, facts-only original-expression basis, dated access record, NIST Copyrights & Disclaimers terms route, attribution, and the declaration that no source expression, media, dataset, table, diagram, or example was reused.

Confirmed lesson-level CC BY 4.0 attribution, accountable author and agent disclosure, an empty third-party-asset ledger, fictional applied settings, and no third-party asset or dataset requiring a separate reuse basis.
limitations: No rendered browser, assistive-technology, MathML, 200% reflow, print, or non-web export test was run; the audit is of canonical Markdown/JSON source and the repository documents state that non-web exporters are not available.

The task prohibited reading site schemas and running npm, so full schema, strict source-preflight, graph, renderer, and repository validation remain operator checks; pack-local JSON, linkage, format, arithmetic, and raw-markup checks passed.

The DLMF robots file could not be independently fetched through the available web interface; the pack's dated checked-no-agent-restriction-found record and reachable NIST terms route were inspected, and no access control or paywall was encountered.
This rights audit applies the project policy and public source record; it is not jurisdiction-specific legal advice.

rationale: The exact original candidate and both eligible advisory artifacts were read in full. The artifacts match PREM-QNT-009 version 0.1.0 and candidate f6c936f1401be42cad2eea2d347e5a1354550cc9, declare the required academic and learning-design roles, use two distinct run IDs and declared providers, contain no declared conflicts, and both preserve request-changes verdicts. Their COMMENTED GitHub transports were supplied by the operator as non-dismissed equivalent submissions but were not independently queried in this run; provenance is therefore disclosed and attested, not verified. All 13 indexed findings are disposed, every substantive change request was incorporated, and the sole no-change-required finding was a numerical confirmation. The revised pack is academically coherent, assessment-aligned, source-traceable, accessible in canonical source, and rights-compliant within the documented limitations. Pack-local structural and arithmetic checks passed, no review artifact changed, and no credible content blocker remains, so merge is warranted subject to final-commit/runtime stamping and canonical operator validation.
dissent: Both eligible advisory reviews requested changes to the original candidate. Those verdicts remain material parts of the record; this decision depends on the indexed finalizer dispositions and one post-review revision, not on representing either reviewer as approving unseen final content.
conditions: Before submitting the adjudication, the operator must replace PENDING-FINAL-COMMIT with the exact 40-character SHA of the final content commit and runtime-stamp the four RUNTIME-STAMPED agent identity fields.
Canonical schema, strict source-preflight, repository, and GitHub-provenance checks must pass after stamping because this run was expressly prohibited from reading site schemas or running npm.

After adjudication, the maintainer may perform only the separately authorized publication transition for lesson status, source confidence, and retained claim review-status fields; no teaching content may change without making this adjudication stale.

adjudicator.displayName: VSBDev — accountable operator and fresh lesson finalizer
